name: string-utils-demo
goal:
Create a Python module called string_utils.py with three simple string utility functions:
1. reverse_string(s) - returns the reversed string
2. count_vowels(s) - returns count of vowels (a,e,i,o,u case-insensitive)
3. is_palindrome(s) - returns True if string is a palindrome (ignoring case and spaces)
implement_instructions: Create the string_utils.py file with the three required functions. Make sure each function handles edge cases like empty strings. Keep the implementation simple and Pythonic.
review_instructions: Verify that all three functions exist and pass the test cases. Return FINAL_STATUS: APPROVED if all checks pass, otherwise FINAL_STATUS: CHANGES_REQUIRED.

checks.name: test-reverse
checks.command: {python} -c "from string_utils import reverse_string; assert reverse_string('hello') == 'olleh'; assert reverse_string('') == ''; print('reverse_string OK')"

checks.name: test-vowels
checks.command: {python} -c "from string_utils import count_vowels; assert count_vowels('hello') == 2; assert count_vowels('AEIOU') == 5; assert count_vowels('xyz') == 0; print('count_vowels OK')"

checks.name: test-palindrome
checks.command: {python} -c "from string_utils import is_palindrome; assert is_palindrome('racecar') == True; assert is_palindrome('A man a plan a canal Panama') == True; assert is_palindrome('hello') == False; print('is_palindrome OK')"
